Verbatim_Snippet: Before an APP entity discloses personal information to an overseas recipient, the entity must take such steps as are reasonable in the circumstances to ensure that the overseas recipient does not breach the APPs (other than APP 1) in relation to the information (APP 8.1).
Mapping_Rationale: This provision establishes a conditional cross-border data transfer regime rather than an outright ban. It requires the disclosing entity to take reasonable steps to ensure the overseas recipient complies with the Australian Privacy Principles and holds the disclosing entity accountable for any breaches by the recipient, unless specific exceptions apply.

APP 8.1: Before an APP entity discloses personal information to an overseas recipient, the entity must take such steps as are reasonable in the circumstances to ensure that the overseas recipient does not breach the APPs (other than APP 1) in relation to the information (APP 8.1).

Verbatim_Snippet: (3) Without limiting subsection (1), if a credit reporting body holds credit reporting information, the body must store the information: (a) either: (i) in Australia or an external Territory; or (ii) in accordance with any security requirements prescribed by the regulations for storing the information outside of Australia and the external Territories; and (b) in accordance with any security requirements prescribed by the regulations.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision imposes a conditional local storage requirement for credit reporting bodies, mandating that credit reporting information be stored within Australia or an external Territory unless specific security requirements prescribed by regulations for offshore storage are met.

Location_Reference: Chapter 8: APP 8 Cross-border disclosure of personal information
Verbatim_Snippet: Before an APP entity discloses personal information to an overseas recipient, the entity must take such steps as are reasonable in the circumstances to ensure that the overseas recipient does not breach the APPs (other than APP 1) in relation to the information (APP 8.1).
Mapping_Rationale: This provision establishes a conditional flow regime by requiring entities to take reasonable steps, such as implementing contractual safeguards, to ensure overseas recipients handle personal information in accordance with the Australian Privacy Principles. Transfers may also proceed without these steps if specific exceptions apply, such as the recipient being subject to a substantially similar law or binding scheme, or upon obtaining the individual's express consent.
